story_clusters.summary: A peace deal has been reached between the US and Iran, which includes the lifting of sanctions on Iran. The deal is expected to have implications for India's economy, including potential benefits for its exports and rupee stability. The opening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route, is also a part of the deal.

articles.title: Outsourced Workers Have No Right To Promotion; Designation Upgrade Without Authority Can Be Corrected: Uttarakhand High Court
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court has held that “ outsourced or contract employees do not automatically acquire the status enjoyed by regular State Employees ”. Thus, holding the above, the High Court iterated that “ continuance of an outsourced employee in service for long periods of time does not Ipso facto establish a direct employee-employer relationship with a State Government ”. articles.title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: IUML enters new era as Fathima Thahiliya becomes first woman MLA
articles.summary: The Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) has elected its first woman MLA, Fathima Thahiliya, in the Kerala Assembly Elections 2026. articles.title: IFSCA (Managing General Agents) Regulations, 2026
articles.summary: The IFSCA (Managing General Agents) Regulations, 2026, have been established to provide a framework for MGAs. The regulations aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and policyholder protection. This move is expected to have a significant impact on the insurance sector in India.
articles.raw_summary: The regulations establish a standalone framework for MGAs, prescribing registration standards, operational safeguards, and supervisory mechanisms. articles.title: Indian officials survey farms around Tata iPhone parts plant after water pollution warning

articles.title: India's fiscal policy is evolving to support domestic growth: S&P Global
articles.summary: India is adjusting its fiscal policy to boost domestic growth and economic strength. Global uncertainties are prompting a move towards long-term strategies. Measures are in place to manage rising energy and food costs. This shift aims to protect growth and reduce vulnerabilities in an uncertain world. India is balancing growth support with long-term stability.

articles.title: From Oman to Tanzania: How the Iran war is redrawing India’s trade map
articles.summary: India's trade is changing due to the West Asia conflict. Oman is now a major import partner, especially for energy. Russia and the US are also key import sources. Singapore has become India's third-largest export destination. Tanzania and South Africa are seeing increased Indian exports. These shifts highlight new trade routes and partnerships.

articles.title: India's export outlook is challenging for FY27, but worst is over: Elara Securities
articles.summary: India's export outlook remains challenging in FY27 amid moderating global demand and elevated electronics imports, but the worst phase appears to be behind as a weaker rupee, resilient services exports and strong remittance inflows are likely to cushion external sector pressures, according to a report by Elara Securities.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal may help boost India's exports, stabilise rupee
articles.summary: A peace deal between the US and Iran is set to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. This agreement is anticipated to significantly boost India's exports to West Asia. It will also help stabilize the Indian rupee and reduce inflationary pressures. The reopening of this vital shipping route promises economic relief and new business opportunities for India.

articles.title: How the US-Iran war changed India’s trade map
articles.summary: India’s hunt for LPG or cooking gas is behind US moving up, while energy also helped imports from Brazil shoot up 2.8 times to $2.7 billion during this period. Shipments from Peru too were 3.7 times higher at over $2 billion, latest numbers from the commerce department showed. The South American nation is now the 20th biggest import source, compared to 35th in April-May 2025.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup fever fuels cyber scams
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are exploiting the FIFA World Cup to scam fans by creating fake ticket portals, merchandise stores, and streaming sites. They are using sophisticated operations to intercept payment details and authentication, and AI to create personalized and convincing phishing campaigns. This is a problem that may lead to policy action to protect consumers and prevent cybercrime.
articles.raw_summary: Cybercriminals are exploiting the FIFA World Cup, creating thousands of fake ticket portals, merchandise stores, and streaming sites to scam fans. Sophisticated operations are intercepting payment details and authentication, while AI is enabling more personalized and convincing phishing campaigns.

articles.title: $320 billion danger: Jefferies warns 70 large EM funds are underweight India
articles.summary: Despite record FII outflows from Indian equities since September 2024, Jefferies said India remains a consensus underweight among emerging market investors. Its analysis of 70 EM funds managing $320 billion showed 61% remain underweight India. Concerns over rich valuations versus growth and uncertainty around the AI/DRAM cycle may delay foreign inflows, though interest is shifting to hard-asset themes.

Highlighting the importance of utilizing food-grade, corrosion-resistant materials, the FSSAI advocates for stringent hygiene measures to avert any forms of contamination.

A peace deal between the US and Iran is anticipated to ease trade further.

articles.context_summary: India achieved a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April 2026. This positive development occurred even with a larger merchandise trade deficit. Stronger net services exports and a significant rise in net transfers contributed to this surplus. Foreign direct investment also saw a substantial increase. However, foreign portfolio investment and banking capital experienced outflows.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has clarified the law on using DNA tests to determine paternity. The court ruled that an individual's right to privacy does not override the need to ascertain biological parentage. This decision will impact individuals seeking to establish paternity and has significant implications for family law in India.

articles.title: No GST on Arbitral Damages: Tata Sons Ruling Clarifies ‘Toleration’
articles.summary: The Court held that damages paid under an arbitral award do not qualify as consideration for a taxable service under GST. This ruling clarifies the 'toleration of an act' provision and its application to compensation payments. The decision is significant for businesses and taxpayers in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that damages paid under an arbitral award do not qualify as consideration for a taxable service under GST. The ruling clarifies that the “toleration of an act” provision requires a separate agreement and cannot cover compensation payments. articles.summary: The Indian government is facing a controversy over presumptive audit, which involves declaring profits below a certain rate. articles.title: Section 34(5) Arbitration Notice: Procedural Requirement or Mandatory condition?
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Section 34(5) is a procedural requirement, not a mandatory precondition for challenging an arbitral award. This means that non-compliance with prior notice requirements does not invalidate a Section 34 petition.

articles.title: ITAT Condones Appeal Delay as Income Tax Portal Glitches Constituted Sufficient Cause
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has ruled that technical glitches on the income tax portal can be considered a valid reason for delaying an appeal. This decision may have implications for taxpayers who have faced similar issues. The ITAT's ruling is a significant development in the context of income tax procedures in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that technical glitches on the income tax portal, coupled with grievances raised by the taxpayer, justified condonation of a 2-month and 21-day delay in filing an appeal. articles.title: Can a Private Limited Company Accept a Loan From a Partnership Firm?
articles.summary: Private limited companies in India cannot accept loans from partnership firms without disclosing it as a deposit. This is according to the Companies (Acceptance of Deposits) Rules, 2014. The loan may be treated as a deposit even if partners are directors of the company.

articles.title: Daily News Wrap-Up: ALMM List-II May Tighten Cell Supply for Module Makers
articles.summary: India’s solar manufacturing sector is bracing for short-term pressure in the cell supply chain, with the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy’s (MNRE) mandating that projects use solar cells from the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) from June 2026. Module manufacturers said that companies without ALMM-listed cell capacity are expected to face constraints in securing compliant cells while they wait for new cell manufacturing facilities to come online over the next few months.
articles.raw_summary: India’s solar manufacturing sector is bracing for short-term pressure in the cell supply chain, with the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy’s (MNRE) mandating that projects use solar cells from the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) from June 2026. Module manufacturers said that companies without ALMM-listed cell capacity are expected to face constraints in securing compliant cells while they wait for new cell manufacturing facilities to come online over the next few months.

articles.title: ‘I only want justice’: bereaved families seek closure one year on from Air India crash

Authorities are focusing on onboarding more banks and simplifying procedures to enhance cross-border transaction efficiency.

articles.title: El Nino may hit India's monsoon, rice and maize output: FAO
articles.summary: India faces a potential threat as El Nino begins. The Food and Agriculture Organization warns of a weakened summer monsoon. The planet's warmer state amplifies these concerns.

articles.title: RBI releases final guidelines on marketing, sales incentives of financial products
articles.summary: RBI has prohibited third-party incentives to employees of regulated entities for selling financial products, while allowing banks and NBFCs to incentivize their own staff. New norms, effective January 1, 2027, aim to prevent aggressive sales and mis-selling, defining digital marketing intermediaries as DSAs/DMAs. Mis-selling will be assessed based on the customer's profile at the time of sale.

articles.title: Remittances, services surplus lift India to $4.7 billion current account surplus in April
articles.summary: India achieved a current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April. This positive shift was driven by increased inward remittances. The services sector also saw a surplus. However, the capital account turned deficit as foreign investors withdrew funds. Despite this, foreign direct investment saw a significant rise. Exporters have adapted to the global economic situation.
articles.raw_summary: India achieved a significant current account surplus of $4.7 billion in April. This positive shift was driven by robust inward remittance flows. The services sector also contributed to the surplus. However, higher imports, particularly crude oil, led to a merchandise deficit. Foreign portfolio investors withdrew funds amid global conflicts, impacting the capital account.

articles.title: Unemployment rate hits 11-month high of 5.5% in May
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to a high in May. Rural joblessness saw a significant rise, reaching a 12-month peak. Urban unemployment eased slightly, particularly for women. Labour force participation and employment rates also saw a moderation. Youth unemployment edged up. These shifts suggest a softening in labour market conditions.

Urban unemployment eased slightly, particularly for women. Labour force participation and employment rates also saw a moderation. Youth unemployment edged up. These shifts suggest a softening in labour market conditions.

articles.title: India introduces PPI for goods and services, begins shift from WPI
articles.summary: India has launched Producer Price Index data for goods and services. This move aims to provide a clearer picture of price changes from a producer's viewpoint. The government plans to phase out Wholesale Price Index numbers within five years. This aligns with international practices and IMF recommendations. The new PPI will offer better insights into inflation experienced by producers.

articles.title: India's unemployment rate rises to 5.5% in May
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate saw an increase in May, reaching 5.5%. This marks a rise from April's 5.2%. Government data indicates a slight uptick in joblessness. Both the Labour Force Participation Rate and Worker Population Ratio moderated. This suggests a softening of labour market conditions. Employment opportunities contracted, leading to more unemployed individuals within the labour force.

Employment opportunities contracted, leading to more unemployed individuals within the labour force.

articles.title: Exports rise 18% to 6-month high in May; trade deficit widens
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports reached a record $45.2 billion in May. Imports also grew, leading to a trade deficit of $28.21 billion. Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal expressed optimism for FY27 if export momentum continues. The West Asia crisis impacted March exports, but recovery is noted. India's export base has expanded significantly over 12 years.

articles.title: USTR arrives June 22 for trade talks; Commerce Secretary says US-Iran deal should bring lasting peace

articles.title: India's trade deficit narrows slightly to $28.21 billion in May as petroleum exports cushion energy import blow

Consequently, global crude oil prices should moderate. This will ease pressure on India's import bill and help control inflation. Exporters anticipate a trade rebound with the Gulf region.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal brings relief, but India turns to a risk at home

articles.title: WPI inflation surges to all-time high of 9.7% under new series
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation hit a record 9.7% in May. This marks the first reading under a new Wholesale Price Index series. Fuel and power prices significantly drove this increase. Manufactured products and primary articles also saw higher inflation. The government also released new price indices for producers and services.

articles.title: Basmati rice, tea exports to West Asia come to a grinding halt
articles.summary: Indian basmati rice and tea exports to West Asia have stopped due to the US-Iran conflict. Vessels carrying rice are waiting at Kandla Port, while tea exporters face unfulfilled orders from GCC countries. This situation has created significant tension and pressure on exporter profitability.

articles.title: India to remain fastest-growing major economy at 6.6% in FY27, down from 7.2% last year: World Bank
articles.summary: India is expected to remain the world’s fastest-growing major economy, with the World Bank projecting GDP growth of 6.6% in FY27, down from 7.7% in FY26 due to higher energy prices and input costs affecting private demand. The report said domestic consumption remains resilient, particularly in rural areas, while GST rate cuts could support spending. Growth is forecast to recover to 7.2% in FY28, aided by stronger domestic demand, export growth, lower US tariffs and free trade agreements.

articles.title: Modi trip a signal about Delhi's reading of a fractured Europe as well as Slovakia

That Modi chose this moment to visit Slovakia, becoming the first Indian prime minister to do so in 33 years, says as much about New Delhi’s reading of a fractured Europe as it does about Bratislava.

articles.title: Heavy selling by foreign funds results in BOP deficit
articles.summary: Net transfers (comprising largely of worker remittances) led the improvement, rising by $6.6 billion to $16 billion from $9.4 billion, reflecting higher remittance inflows in the wake of the West Asia conflict and rupee depreciation. The services surplus also increased by $2.7 billion to $18.6 billion, supported by a rise in exports from $32.8 billion to $37 billion. The primary income deficit narrowed by $1.1 billion to $1.9 billion.
articles.raw_summary: Net transfers (comprising largely of worker remittances) led the improvement, rising by $6.6 billion to $16 billion from $9.4 billion, reflecting higher remittance inflows in the wake of the West Asia conflict and rupee depreciation. The services surplus also increased by $2.7 billion to $18.6 billion, supported by a rise in exports from $32.8 billion to $37 billion. The primary income deficit narrowed by $1.1 billion to $1.9 billion.

articles.title: Razorpay confidentially files for IPO
articles.summary: ​​The Y Combinator and Peak XV Partners-backed company is seeking to raise up to Rs 6,000 crore through the IPO, sources said, adding that the final issue size and timing of launch will depend on market conditions. Razorpay declined to comment. The company was last valued at $7.5 billion. Razorpay, which rivals players such as Cashfree Payments and PayU in India, shifted its domicile from the US to the country in May last year.
